page_content:
BSCI481 Insect Diversity and Classiﬁcation (4 Credits) A summary of the morphology, systematics and evolution of insects and techniques for their collection, preservation and identiﬁcation. Emphasis is placed on the diversity of insects in North America, particularly Maryland and adjacent regions. An insect collection is required. Prerequisite: BSCI337.

BSCI497 Insect Pests of Ornamentals and Turf (4 Credits) The recognition, biology and management of insects and mites injurious to ornamental shrubs, trees, greenhouse crops, and turf. Emphasis on Integrated Pest Management (IPM). Prerequisite: BSCI160 or BSCI337; or (PLSC110 and PLSC111) or (PLSC112 and PLSC113).
